column22:
At 2Impact, we work closely with our clients to develop and implement impactful solutions that bring about real change. From strategy to reporting. And from climate to human rights. Our approach is hands-on and pragmatic.
We are a long-term partner for our clients and work together to devise solutions that really work.
Areas we provide support include:
- Climate (GHG calculation, climate transition plan, SBTi target setting)
- Reporting (CSRD, GRI, Integrated report, impact report)
- Human Rights (human rights due diligence for your value chain, training, risk assessment)
- ESG Ratings (EcoVadis, B Corp, CDP, MSCI, S&P Global, Sustainalytics)
- ESG strategy and policies, Double Materiality Assessment

column20: One idea to help Delaware Consulting become more sustainable is to reduce business travel and use online meetings more often. Consultants often travel to meet clients. Instead, many meetings and workshops can be done using video calls and online collaboration tools. This will reduce flights and car travel, which helps lower pollution. It also saves time and money. In simple words, doing more work online and less traveling can help the company protect the environment.

column22:
Office areas, what can we do more so colleagues in the office work more sustainable?
Making a small A4 paper that we can put in the frames on toilets and a presentation on the TV's including small actions people can do immediately like:
- putting out monitors after work day
- print as less (colored/1-sided) paper as possible
- not letting mails sent to our general info mail address but directly to the group mail of a team (so Office doesn't have to forward mails)
- inform about how to take the stairs from the parking garage in Den Bosch to our office (from tower B taking stairs to the central hall and then take the stairs in tower A to 1st or 3rd floor in Den Bosch to discourage taking the elevator ;-)
etc.

column20: One idea to make the company more sustainable is to invest more in inclusive communication and cultural awareness. A respectful and psychologically safe work environment helps build long-term employee well-being, retention, and collaboration. Small remarks or jokes may sometimes be made without harmful intent, but they can still affect how included colleagues from different cultural backgrounds feel. Raising awareness around this could help create a more supportive and sustainable workplace for everyone.

column20: Today, many of us are experimenting with AI in similar ways. We should accelerate the development of shared AI agents that can be reused across teams, so we can scale knowledge better, avoid duplication, and create greater impact faster. This would also have a positive effect on datacenter usage and therefore on the environment, because it would allow us to use AI more efficiently and less fragmented.

column22: Ecovadis rating
column23:
delaware should be careful not to apply a classical sustainability model to a business that is not classical in nature.
We are a consulting company focused on digital transformation, not a manufacturing or asset-heavy organization.
Our core activity is delivering services for and with clients, often within their own systems, processes, and working models.
That means our sustainability approach must reflect the reality of client-driven delivery.
At the same time, our business model allows a high degree of remote work.
Physical presence should therefore be used with purpose: for client value, collaboration, team bonding, training, and development.
Our biggest levers are not factories or supply chains, but mobility, hybrid work, digital infrastructure, cloud usage, and AI efficiency.
So the goal should not be to copy a standard framework, but to design one that fits who we are.
A relevant and credible approach will be more effective than a generic one.
In short, our sustainability strategy should be tailored to our business model, our people, and the reality of our client work.
